name: Spermidine moved autophagy and immune-senescence markers in older vaccine non-responders
summary: The same pilot links the vaccine-response signal to immune-senescence and autophagy-related B-cell readouts.
statement: Baseline vaccine non-responders had elevated p16, mTOR-signaling, and gamma-H2AX immune-cell signals; after spermidine treatment, those markers fell and B cells showed increased TFEB target and autophagy-related gene expression with elevated autophagic flux.
population_or_model: Adults over 65 years of age after a third SARS-CoV-2 vaccine dose
time_horizon: 2 to 13 weeks, with some markers followed to 37 weeks
caveats: The mechanistic signal was subgroup-concentrated and hypothesis-generating.
Some spermidine and eIF5A hypusination measures showed trends that did not reach statistical significance.
The study measured immune-cell markers and vaccine-response biology, not direct aging reversal.
